page_content:
LARC699 Independent Studies in Landscape and Architecture (1-3 Credits)

Individual Instruction course.

Restriction: Permission of instructor.

Repeatable to: 6 credits.

LARC720 Environmental Analysis and Site Engineering (3 Credits) Techniques for prediction of alterations in social and natural processes brought about by human use of the land; application of such assessments to environmental management; basic methods of landscape alteration, augmentation, and control including grading, drainage, road and trail design, and stormwater management. Prerequisite: LARC640; or permission of AGNR-Plant Science & Landscape Architecture department.
